commands.review_summary:
The PR changes two secrets discovery helpers to seed paths from `resolveDefaultAgentId(config)`, removes the `agentId === "main"` early exits, and adds default-agent path tests.

PR surface: Source -2, Tests +105. Total +103 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. With an empty config or a configured `main` agent and a caller-supplied stateDir that differs from `process.env`, the patched loop falls through to `resolveAgentDir(config, agentId)` and can add an ambient process-state path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR has a concrete stateDir-boundary defect and missing real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while the contributor proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(secrets): use configured default agent ID in auth/model path discovery (#90573)
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace hard-coded `"main"` with `resolveDefaultAgentId(config)` in the two storage-scan helpers that seed path discovery
- Remove the `agentId === "main"` early-exit branches so all configured agents (including `"main"`) resolve through `resolveAgentDir(config, agentId)` uniformly
- Add 8 new unit tests covering default-agent path inclusion and deduplication

## Motivation

Closes #90573.

`listAuthProfileStoreAgentDirs()` and `listAgentModelsJsonPaths()` both hard-coded the string `"main"` in two places each: (1) the initial `paths.add()` seed at the top of the function, and (2) an `agentId === "main"` early-exit branch inside the configured-agents loop.

In multi-agent deployments where the default agent is **not** `"main"` (e.g. `default: true` on a `"nova"` agent), this produced two concrete failures:

1. **Dead path seed** — `agents/main/` was always added even when that directory does not exist and the real default agent lives elsewhere.
2. **Custom agentDir ignored** — the early-exit skipped `resolveAgentDir(config, agentId)` for any agent named `"main"`, so a `"main"` agent with a configured custom `agentDir` would scan the wrong location.

The `resolveDefaultAgentId(config)` helper already implements the correct pattern (reads `cfg.agents.list`, finds the `default: true` entry, falls back to `DEFAULT_AGENT_ID = "main"` when the list is empty). The `resolveMainSessionKey()` call in `main-session.ts` demonstrates the same approach — this change applies it to the two storage helpers that were left behind.

## Verification

- `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean (pre-existing unrelated error in `src/secrets/config-io.ts` not introduced by this PR)
- 8 new tests in `src/secrets/auth-store-paths.default-agent.test.ts` covering:
  - Default fallback to `"main"` when no agents configured
  - Correct path when default agent ID differs from `"main"`
  - All configured agents included
  - Set deduplication (path appears exactly once)
- Did NOT change: `resolveActiveAgentDir()` env-override fallback in `storage-scan.ts` (separate concern, separate issue)
target.author: bartok9
